title: astragalus incanus var. incurvus
intro: Astragalus incanus var. incurvus, also known as the 'curved milkvetch', is a plant species in the legume family that belongs to the genus Astragalus. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Asia, particularly in China, Nepal, and India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, attributed to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. Astragalus incanus var. incurvus is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, and it can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture.
